hero.bio: Network engineer specialized in infrastructure deployment, enterprise networking and automation. Focused on building resilient, observable and secure environments, bridging traditional system administration with modern DevOps practices. Operating personal lab environments to design, break and rebuild infrastructures continuously.
expertise.title: Technical Expertise
expertise.desc: Engineered capability domains — each built through hands-on lab work, production-equivalent configurations and continuous iteration.
lab.title: Homelab Network Topology
lab.desc: Personal infrastructure lab — designed to mirror enterprise environments for testing hardening, automation and observability procedures. Click any node for technical details.
lab.hint: Click or press Enter on any node to view configuration details
projects.title: Active Projects
projects.desc: Engineering-level projects built in personal lab environments. Each represents a focused implementation problem solved end-to-end.
terminal.title: ED-OPS Terminal
career.title: Career & Professional Journey
career.subtitle: Elio Dages — Network & Infrastructure Engineer
career.summary_p1: Bachelor-level IT professional specialized in Systems & Network Administration. Academic background: BTS SIO SISR followed by Bachelor BAIS completed through a work-study program.
career.summary_p2: Strong focus on enterprise infrastructure, deployment automation and network architecture. Professional experience gained at CPAGE within real IT infrastructure environments.
career.cpage_desc: Hands-on infrastructure engineering within enterprise IT environments, focusing on deployment automation, identity management, and operational procedures.
career.cpage_task1: Workstation deployment automation using MDT and custom task sequences
career.cpage_task2: Windows 11 task sequence engineering and systematic customization
career.cpage_task3: Enterprise Active Directory administration and domain management
career.cpage_task4: User lifecycle management — account provisioning, modification, and decommissioning
career.cpage_task5: GPO configuration, security hardening, and compliance policy enforcement
career.cpage_task6: Troubleshooting enterprise environments — workstations, servers, and network components
career.cpage_task7: Support for training infrastructure environments and lab systems
career.cpage_task8: Collaboration with IT teams on infrastructure projects and technical decisions
career.cpage_task9: Documentation maintenance and operational procedure creation
career.phil_infra_desc: Every system decision starts with infrastructure — reliability, segmentation, and observability before application logic.
career.phil_auto_desc: Repetitive tasks should be automated. Every manual operation is a future incident waiting to happen.
career.phil_lab_desc: Personal homelab environment for testing technologies, breaking configurations, and rebuilding with lessons learned.
career.phil_obs_desc: Systems must be observable. Metrics, logs, and alerts are not optional — they are part of the infrastructure.
career.edu_bais_title: Bachelor BAIS — IT Infrastructure & Systems Administration (Bac+3)
career.edu_bais_desc: Bachelor-level degree specializing in Systems & Network Administration, completed through a work-study program at CPAGE. Covers enterprise infrastructure, network architecture, and operational security.
career.edu_bts_title: BTS SIO option SISR — IT Systems & Networks (Bac+2)
career.edu_bts_desc: Two-year technical degree in IT services, option SISR (Infrastructure, Systems and Networks). Foundations in networking, system administration, and security practices.
career.cert_az900_desc: Azure Fundamentals — Cloud concepts & core services
veille.title: Technology Watch & Research
veille.desc: Continuous professional monitoring of IT evolution — tracking enterprise infrastructure trends, security regulations, and cloud transformation.
veille.s1_item1: Hybrid infrastructure architectures — on-premise meets cloud in enterprise environments
veille.s1_item2: Automation trends — infrastructure provisioning moving toward code-defined approaches
veille.s1_item3: Infrastructure as Code adoption — Terraform, Ansible, and configuration management at scale
veille.s2_item1: NIS2 directive impacts on enterprise infrastructure security obligations
veille.s2_item2: Infrastructure security hardening requirements and audit frameworks
veille.s2_item3: Risk management evolution — continuous assessment replacing periodic reviews
veille.s3_item1: Cloud networking concepts — VPC design, hybrid connectivity, cloud-native firewalling
veille.s3_item2: Automation pipelines — CI/CD integration with infrastructure provisioning
veille.s3_item3: Observability at scale — distributed tracing, centralized logging, metrics aggregation
veille.s4_desc: Structured approach to staying current with technology evolution:
veille.s4_item1: Reading official documentation, RFCs, and vendor technical guides
veille.s4_item2: Hands-on experimentation in homelab before adopting new technologies
veille.s4_item3: Testing technologies in isolated lab environments to understand failure modes
veille.s4_item4: Technical curiosity mindset — questioning assumptions and exploring edge cases
terminal.cmd.career.name: Elio Dages
terminal.cmd.career.focus: Deployment automation, AD administration, GPO hardening
